description: Quick-reference companion for task-plan-generate.xml — pre-distilled tables, formulas, and constraints for goal generation.

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by tmux-cli — do not edit. Changes will be overwritten on next tmux-cli start. -->

You are running autonomous goal generation from validated discovery outputs. This companion provides pre-computed reference data so you do not need to re-derive formulas, ordering, or constraints from the full XML. Read this first, then follow the XML flow.

## Discovery output files

| File | Key data extracted in Step 0 |
|------|------------------------------|
| `bounded-contexts.md` | BC_LIST (### headings), AGGREGATE_MAP (per-BC aggregate names) |
| `domain-model.md` | Per-aggregate: VOs, events, services, repo interfaces, read models, invariants |
| `context-map.md` | Cross-BC deps: upstream BCs, relationship types, ACL interfaces (multi-BC only) |
| `api-endpoints.md` | ENDPOINT_MAP: per-BC endpoints partitioned into commands (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) and queries (GET) |
| `cross-cutting.md` | Auth flows, event listeners, middleware, API docs, health check decisions |
| `test-environment.md` | Base URL, credentials, DB name, fixture command, Playwright status |
| `adrs/` | Architecture decisions: Redis, RabbitMQ, Docker, CI/CD, Messenger transport selections |

## Goal count formula

```
goals = 1 (gate 0) + 1 (scaffold)
      + Σ per BC: 3 (domain + app + infra) + N_actions
      + 1 (fixtures) + N_auth_flows + N_event_listeners + 1 (error handling)
      + [1 HTTP middleware] + [1 API docs] + [1 Messenger] + 1 (health check)
      + 1 (Makefile/DX) + [1 Docker] + [1 CI/CD]
      + 3 (final: Deptrac + E2E + quality)
```

Bracketed items are conditional — include only when selected in ADRs/discovery. Auth endpoints count in N_auth_flows only, NOT in per-BC N_actions (G-09, no double counting).

`[1 Docker]` is conditional on `RUN_TARGET=docker OR a deployment ADR`: the compose+ports goal (step 3.26) fires whenever `RUN_TARGET=docker` (read from `test-environment.md`) — a deployment ADR is NOT required — and also when a Docker/container/deployment ADR is present. When both hold, exactly ONE goal is emitted (the ADR only adds production extras). Its host:container port mappings and `APP_PORT` come from the `Published Ports` block in `test-environment.md`.

## Phase ordering chain

```
goal-001 Gate 0 → goal-002 Scaffold
  → goal-003..N  Per-BC domain (each depends on scaffold)
  → goal-N+1..M  Per-BC application (each depends on own BC's domain, G-04)
  → goal-M+1..P  Per-BC infrastructure (each depends on own BC's application)
  → goal-P+1     Fixtures (depends on ALL infra goals, G-03)
  → goal-P+2     Error handling (depends on fixtures — needed before action E2E, G-04b)
  → goal-P+3..Q  Auth flows (depends on fixtures + error handling)
  → goal-Q+1..R  Controller actions (depends on own BC infra + fixtures + error + auth)
  → goal-R+1..S  Event listeners (depends on relevant BC infra + fixtures)
  → goal-S+1..T  Cross-cutting (middleware, API docs, Messenger, health, Makefile)
  → goal-T+1..T+2 Docker, CI/CD (conditional)
  → last 3       Final gates: Deptrac, E2E regression, quality (G-08)
```

Error handling before actions: action E2E tests assert RFC 7807 errors (422/400). Auth before actions: action E2E tests assert 401/403.

## Investigation Types (condensed)

| Phase | Inv 1 | Inv 2 | Inv 3 | Inv 4 |
|-------|-------|-------|-------|-------|
| Gate 0 (local) | PHP + extensions | Composer | Node/npm (cond.) | DB + services (cond.) |
| Gate 0 (docker) | Docker engine + run | Compose v2 | DB + services (if external) | (none) |
| Scaffold | bin/console boots | DDD dirs correct | PHPStan clean | Test suite green |
| Domain | DDD violations (imports, constructors, VOs) | Invariant audit | Unit tests + coverage | PHPStan 9 |
| Application | Domain leak (no infra/HTTP) | Handler contracts | Unit tests + coverage | PHPStan 9 |
| Infrastructure | Doctrine schema + DBAL types | Migration safety + flush-dispatch | Integration tests (real DB) | Deptrac + ACL |
| Fixtures | Load without error | Users match test-env | Sufficient list/page data | Idempotent |
| Action | No domain logic in controller | Deptrac (ctrl→App only) | PHPStan 9 + ECS | Playwright E2E (3 attempts) |
| Auth | All flows implemented | Permission matrix tested | PHPStan 9 + ECS | Playwright auth E2E |
| Event listener | Wiring + ACL cross-BC | Handler isolation | Integration tests | PHPStan 9 |
| Error handling | Exception types mapped | RFC 7807 format | No internals on 500 | PHPStan + ECS |
| Final Deptrac | Full codebase scan | Layer matrix verified | No cross-BC in Domain/App | ACL-only cross-BC in Infra |
| Final E2E | All Playwright tests | No order-dependent failures | Status codes correct | Response bodies valid |
| Final Quality | PHPStan 9 full | ECS full | Unit + integration green | Coverage >= threshold |

Each goal needs 2-4 investigators (GM-08) with type, files, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

## Common pitfalls from AC tables

| AC ID | Constraint | Typical violation |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|
| G-01 | Gate 0 is always goal-001 | Numbering starts at 0 or skips |
| G-03 | Fixtures depends on ALL infra goals | Only depends on last infra goal |
| G-04 | Per-BC order: domain→app→infra strict | App goal skips domain dependency |
| G-05 | Every controller action = separate goal | Actions batched into one goal |
| G-08 | Last 3 goals are final gates | Cross-cutting goals placed after finals |
| G-11 | Every goal has max_retries >= 3 | max_retries omitted or set to 1 |
| G-16 | Read from docs/architecture/* only | Using conversation context for inputs |
| G-18 | No fix goals — retries reuse same goal | Creating goal-NNN-fix entries |
| GM-05 | E2E goals reference test-environment.md | Credentials duplicated into goal.md |
| GM-08 | 2-4 investigators per goal | Only 1 investigator or more than 4 |
| GM-10 | Paths follow src/{BC}/Domain|App|Infra/ | Flat src/ structure without DDD layers |
| GM-12 | Action goal: request DTO, response DTO, controller, route, E2E test | Missing Playwright test deliverable |

## Structured validate/acceptance/scope mandate (CRITICAL)

Every goal-creation call (`goal-create` MCP or `tmux-cli taskvisor goal add`) MUST pass **structured** params:

- `validate`: >= 1 concrete, project-runnable command resolved against the detected stack (e.g. `vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Booking/`, `go test ./internal/booking/`, `npx playwright test tests/e2e/booking.spec.ts`) — never prose.
- `acceptance`: >= 1 criterion.
- `scope`: path globs whenever the file footprint is known. Empty scope ⇒ the goal serializes against ALL concurrent goals (DisjointReadySet gate) — scope is the price of parallelism.

Validation rules written only as prose in goal.md are **NOT sufficient** — the daemon reads only the structured fields. Prose-only rules produce `validate: None`, which makes the dispatch-time repair inject wrong default investigators (guaranteed validation failures in non-Go projects → budget-exhaustion cascade).

## Fan-out patterns quick reference

| Goal type | Pattern | Task structure |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| Scaffold | Multi-task (3) | task-0: composer → task-1: DDD dirs (depends 0) → task-2: configs (depends 0) |
| Domain (>1 agg) | Multi-task | task-0: shared (trait, base exception, ACL) → task-1..N: per aggregate (parallel) |
| Domain (1 agg) | Single-task | All deliverables in one task |
| Application | Single-task | One task per BC (handlers share ports — splitting duplicates mocks) |
| Infrastructure (>3 entities) | Multi-task | task-0: services.yaml + DBAL types → task-1..N: per aggregate mapping+repo |
| Infrastructure (<=3) | Single-task | All mappings, repos, tests in one task |
| Action | Single-task | One goal per action: controller + DTO + route + E2E test (G-05) |
| Final quality | Multi-task | Parallel: PHPStan, ECS, Deptrac, test suites |

File-disjoint rule: parallel tasks within a goal must have non-overlapping deliverable files. Shared files go in a prerequisite task-0.

## Share type ownership

DomainEventTrait and base DomainException are always owned by the FIRST BC processed — assigned to task-0 of that BC's domain goal. BC-specific Share types (DataTypes, Events) are owned by the first BC that introduces them. Subsequent BCs reference but do not include them in deliverables.

Follow `.claude/commands/tmux/task-plan-generate.xml` EXACTLY as written. That file defines the complete generation flow, all validation gates, and hard constraints. Do not improvise beyond it.
